X.essay: Solid capability-matrix RFC with centralized middleware and clear phasing. Faithful to Free/Pro intent but slightly reinterprets plan codes (monthly/yearly as plan codes) and leans on middleware in a codebase that often prefers typed in-handler errors. Edge coverage is good; async quota commit is thinner than the stronger candidate.

Y.essay: Excellent PRD-faithful architecture: subscriptions + usage ledger with reserve/commit/release, Entitlement/Usage/Billing services, async job commit rules, and MVP phases M1–M3. Deep fit to Effect Schema, Drizzle, AppLayer, and existing generate/export seams. Highly implementable with concrete flows and tests.
